quote: You have power over your mind - not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
author: Marcus Aurelius, Meditations

quote: The unexamined life is not worth living.
author: Socrates, Apology

quote: To be, or not to be, that is the question.
author: William Shakespeare, Hamlet

quote: Man is not, properly speaking, good, but he is educated to be good.
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky, The Brothers Karamazov

quote: That which does not kill us makes us stronger.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche, Twilight of the Idols

quote: It is never too late to be what you might have been.
author: George Eliot, Middlemarch

quote: The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing.
author: Socrates (via Plato)

quote: Whatever you are, be a good one.
author: Abraham Lincoln

quote: In the midst of winter, I found there was, within me, an invincible summer.
author: Albert Camus, Return to Tipasa

quote: Beauty is truth, truth beauty, that is all ye know on earth.
author: John Keats, Ode on a Grecian Urn

quote: He who has a why to live for can bear almost any how.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche, Twilight of the Idols

quote: The journey of a thousand miles begins with one step.
author: Lao Tzu, Tao Te Ching

quote: Pain and suffering are always inevitable for a large intelligence and a deep heart.
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky, Crime and Punishment

quote: The only way to have a friend is to be one.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson, Essays

quote: All that is gold does not glitter, not all those who wander are lost.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien, The Fellowship of the Ring

quote: Happiness depends upon ourselves.
author: Aristotle, Nicomachean Ethics

quote: I think, therefore I am.
author: Rene Descartes, Discourse on the Method

quote: Not all who wander are lost, but some certainly are searching.
author: Cheryl Strayed, Wild

quote: Darkness cannot drive out darkness; only light can do that. Hate cannot drive out hate; only love can do that.
author: Martin Luther King Jr., Strength to Love

quote: To be yourself in a world that is constantly trying to make you something else is the greatest accomplishment.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson, Self-Reliance

quote: It was the best of times, it was the worst of times.
author: Charles Dickens, A Tale of Two Cities

quote: Man is born free, and everywhere he is in chains.
author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau, The Social Contract

quote: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
author: Will Durant (summarizing Aristotle)

quote: The only thing we have to fear is fear itself.
author: Franklin D. Roosevelt, First Inaugural Address

quote: So it goes.
author: Kurt Vonnegut, Slaughterhouse-Five

quote: Two things fill the mind with ever new and increasing admiration and awe: the starry heavens above me and the moral law within me.
author: Immanuel Kant, Critique of Practical Reason

quote: It does not do to dwell on dreams and forget to live.
author: J.K. Rowling, Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone

quote: Love all, trust a few, do wrong to none.
author: William Shakespeare, All's Well That Ends Well

quote: To love another person is to see the face of God.
author: Victor Hugo, Les Miserables

quote: A man is but the product of his thoughts. What he thinks, he becomes.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

quote: The cave you fear to enter holds the treasure you seek.
author: Joseph Campbell, The Hero with a Thousand Faces

quote: Whatever our souls are made of, his and mine are the same.
author: Emily Bronte, Wuthering Heights

quote: One must still have chaos in oneself to give birth to a dancing star.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche, Thus Spoke Zarathustra

quote: There is nothing either good or bad, but thinking makes it so.
author: William Shakespeare, Hamlet

quote: The mind is everything. What you think you become.
author: Buddha (attributed)

quote: To live is to suffer, to survive is to find some meaning in the suffering.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

quote: I am not afraid of storms, for I am learning how to sail my ship.
author: Louisa May Alcott, Little Women

quote: Man is condemned to be free.
author: Jean-Paul Sartre, Existentialism is a Humanism

quote: The privilege of a lifetime is to become who you truly are.
author: Carl Jung

quote: It is only with the heart that one can see rightly; what is essential is invisible to the eye.
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupery, The Little Prince

quote: Life must be understood backward. But it must be lived forward.
author: Soren Kierkegaard

quote: There is some good in this world, and it's worth fighting for.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien, The Two Towers

quote: The struggle itself toward the heights is enough to fill a man's heart.
author: Albert Camus, The Myth of Sisyphus

quote: Whereof one cannot speak, thereof one must be silent.
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein, Tractatus Logico-Philosophicus

quote: You must be the change you wish to see in the world.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

quote: The world breaks everyone, and afterward, some are strong at the broken places.
author: Ernest Hemingway, A Farewell to Arms

quote: I have measured out my life with coffee spoons.
author: T.S. Eliot, The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock

quote: Freedom is what you do with what's been done to you.
author: Jean-Paul Sartre

quote: Only in the agony of parting do we look into the depths of love.
author: George Eliot, Middlemarch

quote: The wound is the place where the Light enters you.
author: Rumi
